questions.question: List and briefly explain the five framework activities in software engineering.
keyPoints: Communication - involves understanding stakeholder needs and gathering requirements
Planning - involves estimating costs, scheduling, and risk management
Modeling - involves analysis and design of the system
Construction - involves coding and testing the software
Deployment - involves delivering software to customers and ensuring proper operation

questions.question: What are the two components of Modeling activity? Explain each briefly.
keyPoints: Analysis - understanding and representing requirements from stakeholder perspective
Design - creating system architecture and structure to meet requirements

questions.question: Explain the role of risk management in the Planning activity.
keyPoints: Identifying potential problems that could affect the project
Assessing the likelihood and impact of risks
Developing mitigation strategies and contingency plans

questions.question: What does the Construction activity encompass? Explain the key components.
keyPoints: Code generation - writing actual program code
Unit testing - testing individual components
Integration testing - testing combined modules
Debugging - fixing errors found during testing

questions.question: How does Communication activity differ from other framework activities?
keyPoints: It focuses on understanding stakeholder needs rather than technical aspects
It establishes relationships and gathers requirements
It occurs before planning and continues throughout the project

questions.question: Explain the purpose of Deployment activity in the software engineering process.
keyPoints: Delivering the software product to the customer
Installing and configuring the software in the operational environment
Providing user training and support
Gathering feedback for future improvements

questions.question: What is the relationship between framework activities and software engineering principles?
keyPoints: Principles guide each framework activity systematically
They ensure quality and consistency in software development
They provide a disciplined approach to software engineering

questions.question: Describe in detail the five framework activities of software engineering, explaining what each activity involves and its outputs.
keyPoints: Communication: Stakeholder interaction, requirement gathering, project initiation - Output: Requirements document
Planning: Project estimation, task definition, scheduling, risk management - Output: Project plan
Modeling: Analysis (requirements model) and Design (architecture, database, interface design) - Output: Design documents
Construction: Code development, unit testing, integration testing - Output: Tested software code
Deployment: Installation, configuration, user training, maintenance setup - Output: Operational software

questions.question: Explain how the framework activities are interconnected. Why is it important to follow this sequence in software development?
keyPoints: Communication feeds into Planning by providing requirements
Planning provides estimates that guide Modeling
Modeling produces designs that direct Construction
Construction creates product for Deployment
Following sequence ensures: proper understanding before building, systematic approach, reduced rework, better quality, stakeholder satisfaction

questions.question: Discuss the principles that guide each framework activity in software engineering.
keyPoints: Communication: Collaborative engagement, requirement elicitation, stakeholder analysis
Planning: Realistic estimation, risk-driven planning, iterative refinement
Modeling: Abstraction, multiple views, design for quality
Construction: Coding standards, thorough testing, quality focus
Deployment: User-centric delivery, operational support, continuous improvement
Overall: Systematic, disciplined approach ensuring quality software

questions.question: A software project is about to start. Using the framework activities, outline the steps you would follow from project initiation to deployment.
keyPoints: Phase 1 - Communication: Meet stakeholders, gather requirements, establish communication channels
Phase 2 - Planning: Estimate costs, define milestones, create schedule, identify risks
Phase 3 - Modeling: Create analysis models (use cases, domain models), develop design models (architecture, class diagrams)
Phase 4 - Construction: Write code, perform unit testing, integration testing, system testing
Phase 5 - Deployment: Install software, configure environment, train users, release to production
